mnemonics: USE Super for a Super-Quick Setup
keyPoints: A custom exception must extend the Exception class (for checked) or RuntimeException (for unchecked).
Provide constructors that call the superclass constructor, often using super(message).
Throw the custom exception in your code using the 'throw' keyword when a specific error condition is met.
commonMistakes: Extending the wrong class (e.g., Throwable directly) instead of Exception or RuntimeException.
Forgetting to add a constructor that takes a String message parameter, making the exception harder to debug.
Creating overly broad custom exceptions instead of specific ones for different error scenarios.
